claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm asking about my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'd like to ask you some questions about Alex to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Since Alex is 17 years old, **is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he studying at school, college, or undertaking an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you.

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing that information.

Just a couple more questions: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question: **Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?**

(This won't affect your eligibility for Child Benefit itself, but it may mean you have to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit even if your income is over this amount.)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you, making you responsible for him
- Although Alex is 17 (over the usual age limit of 16), you still qualify because he's under 20 and is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Since your income is below £60,000, you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you'll need to make a claim to start receiving the benefit.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). Under the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you can still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex, and is responsible for him. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is 17 and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant's income is below £60,000, so they will not face the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). Under the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you can still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex, and is responsible for him. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is 17 and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant's income is below £60,000, so they will not face the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
